message:
feat(abi): add 11 stdio symbols with full test coverage (getc, putc, fgetpos, fsetpos, fdopen, freopen, remove, getdelim, getline, tmpfile, tmpnam)

Implements bd-2vv.3 stdio family expansion:
- getc/putc: function versions of fgetc/fputc
- fgetpos/fsetpos: stream position save/restore via opaque fpos_t
- fdopen: wrap existing fd as FILE stream
- freopen: close and reopen stream with new file
- remove: unlink file (falls back to rmdir for directories)
- getdelim/getline: dynamic line reading with malloc/realloc
- tmpfile: anonymous temp file via O_TMPFILE with mkstemp fallback
- tmpnam: unique temp filename generation with atomic counter
- 14 new integration tests (29 total stdio tests, all passing)
- Updated libc.map version script and support_matrix.json

Counts: total_exported=482, implemented=306, raw_syscall=172, glibc_call_through=4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vv.10","title":"Trace-weighted symbol tiers + family wave roadmap","description":"Background:
- Symbol closure must prioritize practical runtime impact.

Scope:
- Build workload-frequency weighted prioritization tiers and map them to implementation waves.
- Define family-level wave milestones with mandatory test/log requirements.

Deliverables:
1) Tiered symbol priority model (Top50/Top200/Top500/etc.).
2) Family wave roadmap with owners and dependencies.
3) Wave acceptance checklist.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Priority tiers are trace-backed and reproducible.
- Wave plan aligns with replacement-level goals.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for tiering logic.
- E2E trace replay to regenerate tiers.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, tier, family, planned_wave, rationale.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.521623572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.505383902Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.505278525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["roadmap","symbols","traces"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.521623572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:30.266458840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.11","title":"Cross-report consistency gate for support/reality/replacement claims","description":"Background:
- Classification and prioritization must stay consistent with release claims and reports.

Scope:
- Add consistency gate across support_matrix, reality_report, replacement_levels, and release claims.
- Enforce no-unknown/no-forbidden states for declared target tiers.

Deliverables:
1) Cross-report consistency checker.
2) Claim policy checks tied to replacement levels.
3) CI enforcement and triage outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Inconsistent claims fail CI deterministically.
- Report drift is caught before release artifacts are signed.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for consistency rules.
- E2E intentional-drift scenarios.
- Logs: trace_id, report_pair, inconsistency_type, affected_symbols, verdict.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.634971717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.659030707Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:27:21.658962148Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","claims","symbols"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:28.634971717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:30.370599528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.2","title":"Symbol expansion: stdlib family completion","description":"Complete stdlib family coverage.

Current State:
- Conversion functions (atoi, strtol, etc.): partial Implemented
- Search/sort (qsort, bsearch): partial Implemented
- Environment (getenv, setenv): see stub wave 1
- Random (rand, random, arc4random): partial
- Exit handlers (atexit, exit): partial
- Temp files (mkstemp, tmpnam): likely stubs

Target Additions:
- strtoimax, strtoumax (intmax conversions)
- realpath (path canonicalization)
- mkdtemp (temp directory)
- secure_getenv
- All *_l locale variants
- posix_memalign, aligned_alloc

Implementation Notes:
- realpath needs filesystem access (path resolution)
- Random functions need entropy source decision
- Exit handlers need registration chain
- Locale variants need locale infrastructure

Testing:
- Unit: boundary values for conversions (INT_MAX, overflow)
- Unit: qsort stability, bsearch edge cases
- E2E: programs using realpath, mkstemp
- Hardened: safe defaults for random seed

Success Criteria:
- All stdlib.h functions classified
- Conversion functions match glibc precision
- Exit handler order matches glibc

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmBadger","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:05.221807805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:51:57.881444828Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","stdlib"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.2","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.312910231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.419440234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:05.221807805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":408,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16): Completed first implementation slice for stdlib family completion by adding `strtoimax` and `strtoumax` end-to-end.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/conversion.rs
  - Added `strtoimax_impl`/`strtoimax` and `strtoumax_impl`/`strtoumax` aliases over existing conversion machinery.
  - Added unit tests: `test_strtoimax_aliases_strtol`, `test_strtoumax_aliases_strtoul`.
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/mod.rs
  - Exported `strtoimax` and `strtoumax`.
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs
  - Added ABI exports: `strtoimax` and `strtoumax` with membrane/risk-policy handling and `endptr` semantics matching existing strto* ABI implementations.
- support_matrix.json
  - Added symbols `strtoimax` and `strtoumax` as `Implemented` in `stdlib_abi`.
  - Updated totals: `total_exported` 255 -> 257, `Implemented` 152 -> 154.

Verification (all offloaded via rch):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core stdlib::conversion -- --nocapture` ✅ (12 passed, 0 failed)

Next planned slice for bd-2vv.2:
- Expand stdlib conversion/env coverage with one or more of: `secure_getenv`, `realpath`, `mkdtemp` (bounded implementation + tests + matrix update).
","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:21:46Z"},{"id":409,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16, slice 2): added `secure_getenv` coverage.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs
  - Added GNU `secure_getenv` export.
  - Behavior: returns `NULL` under secure execution heuristic (`uid!=euid || gid!=egid`), otherwise delegates to existing membrane-validated `getenv` path.
- support_matrix.json
  - Added `secure_getenv` as `Implemented` (`stdlib_abi`).
  - Updated totals: `total_exported` 257 -> 258, `Implemented` 154 -> 155.

Verification (all offloaded via rch):
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

Notes:
- This keeps progress incremental and bounded inside `bd-2vv.2` while expanding stdlib API surface without introducing new host-glibc call-through dependency.

Progress update (CalmBadger, 2026-02-16, slice 3): added stdlib conversion symbols atoll, strtoll, and strtoull end-to-end.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/conversion.rs
  - added atoll, strtoll_impl/strtoll, strtoull_impl/strtoull
  - added unit tests for alias behavior
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/mod.rs
  - exported atoll, strtoll, strtoull

  - added ABI exports atoll/strtoll/strtoull via existing validated conversion paths
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdlib_abi_test.rs
  - added integration tests:
    - atoll_parses_i64_limits
    - strtoll_sets_endptr_to_first_unparsed_byte
    - strtoull_sets_endptr_to_first_unparsed_byte
- support_matrix.json

Validation evidence (cargo-heavy commands offloaded via rch):
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets : PASS

bd-2vv.2 remains in progress for additional stdlib coverage slices.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:51:57Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vv.3","title":"Symbol expansion: stdio family (phased approach)","description":"Complete stdio family coverage - the largest and most complex subsystem.

Current State:
- FILE* operations: mostly GlibcCallThrough
- printf family: partial Implemented (format engine exists)
- scanf family: likely stubs
- fopen/fclose/fread/fwrite: GlibcCallThrough

Target Architecture:
FrankenLibC needs its own FILE structure and buffering:
1. FILE state machine (read/write/append modes)
2. Buffer management (fully buffered, line buffered, unbuffered)
3. Error and EOF flag handling
4. Wide character support (fwide, fwprintf)

Implementation Phases:
Phase 1: Core FILE operations
- fopen, fclose, fflush
- fread, fwrite
- fgetc, fputc, fgets, fputs

Phase 2: Formatted I/O
- printf family (fprintf, sprintf, snprintf, asprintf)
- scanf family (fscanf, sscanf)

Phase 3: Positioning
- fseek, ftell, fgetpos, fsetpos
- rewind

Phase 4: Misc
- freopen, fdopen, fileno
- setvbuf, setbuf
- clearerr, feof, ferror

Testing:
- Unit: each FILE operation in isolation
- Integration: multi-step file processing workflows
- Concurrency: thread-safe FILE operations
- Hardened: handling of invalid FILE pointers

Complexity Warning:
This is ~20% of glibc by LOC. Needs phased approach.

Success Criteria:
- All stdio.h functions classified Implemented
- Zero glibc call-through for stdio
- Python hello world works (heavy stdio user)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

 {"id":"bd-2vv.3.1","title":"Stdio Phase 1: FILE structure and basic I/O","description":"Stdio Phase 1: Core FILE structure and basic operations.

Goal: Implement FrankenLibC's own FILE structure with basic I/O.

FILE Structure Design:
- File descriptor (int fd)
- Buffer pointer and size
- Buffer position (read/write)
- Flags (read/write/append/binary)
- Error and EOF flags
- Lock for thread safety
- Wide orientation flag

Phase 1 Functions:
- fopen, fclose, fflush
- fread, fwrite
- fgetc, fputc, ungetc
- fgets, fputs
- fileno

Buffer Management:
- Full buffering (_IOFBF)
- Line buffering (_IOLBF)
- No buffering (_IONBF)
- setvbuf, setbuf

Standard Streams:
- stdin, stdout, stderr initialization
- Connection to fd 0, 1, 2

Testing:
- Unit: each operation in isolation
- Integration: read/write round-trips
- Concurrency: concurrent access to same FILE
- Error: invalid FILE pointer handling

Success Criteria:
- Basic file I/O works
- Buffer management correct
- Thread safety maintained

## Acceptance Criteria

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdio_abi_test.rs`
  - Added negative/edge-path tests:
    - `rejects_invalid_open_mode_and_null_stream_handles`
    - `null_and_zero_length_io_paths_are_safe_defaults`
    - `fgets_rejects_invalid_destination_or_size`
  - Existing phase-1 round-trip tests retained for:
    - `fopen/fputs/fflush/fclose`

    - `fwrite/fread`

    - `fileno/setvbuf/setbuf`

Notes:
- This pass focused on deterministic ABI test expansion and strict failure-path behavior.
- Remaining closure work for full bead acceptance still includes broader integration/e2e fixture evidence + structured log artifact indexing.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T20:27:34Z"},{"id":427,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3.1","author":"BeigePeak","text":"Progress update (BeigePeak, 2026-02-16): added deterministic stdio phase-1 fixture/e2e evidence lane and validated strict+hardened execution artifacts.

Implementation surfaces:
- `tests/integration/fixture_stdio.c`
  - Added fixture coverage for: `fopen`, `fclose`, `fflush`, `fread`, `fwrite`, `fgetc`, `fputc`, `fgets`, `fputs`, `ungetc`, `fileno`, `setvbuf`, `setbuf`.
  - Includes deterministic happy-path + error-path checks and prints `fixture_stdio: PASS` on success.
- `tests/conformance/c_fixture_spec.json`
  - Added `fixture_stdio` spec entry with strict/hardened mode expectations and traceability refs.
  - Updated fixture/spec summary totals and required acceptance symbol coverage.
- `scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh`
  - Added structured `trace.jsonl` emission with fields: `timestamp`, `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
  - Added deterministic `artifact_index.json` generation with sha256 + size metadata.
  - Added `FIXTURE_FILTER` env support for bead-scoped fixture runs.

Validation (all CPU-heavy ops via `rch exec`):
- `rch exec -- bash scripts/check_c_fixture_suite.sh` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test c_fixture_suite_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (8 passed)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdio_abi_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (8 passed)

 {"id":"bd-2vv.3.2","title":"Stdio Phase 2: Printf family","description":"Stdio Phase 2: Printf family implementation.

Goal: Complete printf format engine.

Printf Functions:
- printf, fprintf, sprintf, snprintf
- vprintf, vfprintf, vsprintf, vsnprintf
- asprintf, vasprintf (GNU extensions)
- dprintf, vdprintf

Format Specifiers:
- Integer: d, i, u, o, x, X, b (C23)
- Float: f, F, e, E, g, G, a, A
- Character: c, s, p, n, %
- Width and precision: *, digit
- Flags: -, +, space, #, 0
- Length modifiers: hh, h, l, ll, j, z, t, L

Implementation Strategy:
Format engine in frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs:
1. Parse format string
2. Extract arguments from va_list
3. Format each specifier
4. Output to destination

Complexity Points:
- Floating-point formatting (Ryu or Dragonbox)
- Positional arguments ($)
- Wide character output (wprintf family)

Testing:
- Unit: each specifier in isolation
- Edge cases: width overflow, precision truncation
- Locale: decimal separator (depends on locale)
- Security: %n restrictions in hardened mode

Success Criteria:
- All specifiers implemented
- Output matches glibc format
- Hardened mode restricts %n

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BeigePeak","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:39.627717731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T23:07:31.809599927Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase2","printf","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:39.627717731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:06:08.232954796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":430,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3.2","author":"BeigePeak","text":"Progress slice landed: deterministic printf-family e2e fixture coverage and validation evidence.

 {"id":"bd-2vv.4","title":"Symbol expansion: math family with precision validation","description":"Complete math family coverage with correct rounding.

Current State:
- Basic functions (sin, cos, exp, log): likely Implemented via libm
- Special functions (gamma, bessel): likely stubs
- Float variants (sinf, sinl): partial
- FP environment (fenv): partial

Implementation Strategy:
Most math can delegate to Rust's libm crate or system libm.
Key is ensuring:
1. Correct rounding modes
2. NaN/Inf handling
3. errno setting (EDOM, ERANGE)
4. Exception flags (FE_DIVBYZERO, etc.)

Target Functions:
- Trig: sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2
- Hyperbolic: sinh, cosh, tanh, asinh, acosh, atanh
- Exponential: exp, exp2, expm1, log, log2, log10, log1p
- Power: pow, sqrt, cbrt, hypot
- Special: lgamma, tgamma, erf, erfc, j0, j1, jn, y0, y1, yn
- Rounding: ceil, floor, trunc, round, nearbyint, rint
- Remainder: fmod, remainder, remquo
- FP manipulation: frexp, ldexp, modf, scalbn, copysign
- Classification: fpclassify, isnan, isinf, isfinite, isnormal
- FP environment: fegetround, fesetround, feclearexcept, etc.

Testing:
- Unit: boundary values (±0, ±Inf, NaN, denormals)
- Precision: compare with mpfr for accuracy
- Rounding modes: test all four modes
- Errno: verify correct errno setting

Notes:
- float/double/long double variants (f, l suffixes)
- Some functions are macros in glibc (type-generic)

Success Criteria:
- All math.h functions classified Implemented
- Precision within 1 ULP of reference
- Correct errno/exception behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
